original_abstract: Since the first demonstrations of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis in 2006, these bifunctional chiral scaffolds have emerged as highly effective tools for promoting a wide array of enantioselective transformations. By combining the unique activation modes of primary amines with the hydrogen-bonding capabilities of thioureas, researchers have successfully synthesized a diverse range of versatile organic compounds with high optical purity. This review summarizes the remarkable progress made in the field, detailing the development of various catalyst frameworks and their applications in key reactions such as nitro-Michael additions, conjugate additions to unsaturated carbonyls, Mannich photochemistry, and diverse domino or multicomponent processes.
humanized_abstract: From 2006, when the first examples of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysts were first shown to catalyze asymmetric reactions with high enantioselectivity, the field of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis has grown to become a significant area of research for synthesizing hundreds of chiral organic molecules of significant structural complexity and diversity. Building on the synergistic pairing of primary amine activation modes with the thiourea hydrogen-bond donating and accepting capabilities, multifaceted catalyst structures have been synthesized that facilitate the efficient catalysis of an ever increasing number of chiroselective reactions. This review article covers the achievements in this field focusing on catalyst structures, mechanism, scope and applications of catalytic nitro-Michael additions, conjugate additions to activated alkenes and alkynes, as well as on Mannich type photochemistry and domino/multicat multi-component reactions.

Building on the synergistic pairing of primary amine activation modes with the thiourea hydrogen-bond donating and accepting capabilities, multifaceted catalyst structures have been synthesized that facilitate the efficient catalysis of an ever increasing number of chiroselective reactions. This review article covers the achievements in this field focusing on catalyst structures, mechanism, scope and applications of catalytic nitro-Michael additions, conjugate additions to activated alkenes and alkynes, as well as on Mannich type photochemistry and domino/multicat multi-component reactions.
